contracts.shape: Typed PASS, FAIL, BLOCKED, paused, cancelled, or ready-to-merge result carrying release/track/slice identity, durable status revision, Git effect reference, operation-event ID, and notification-enqueue result. A missing required effect is a non-PASS persistence error; readiness is distinct from merge success.
contracts.live_test: S12 drives the real binary through PASS, BLOCKED, cancellation, restart, and ready-to-merge outcomes and reconciles each record to Git and the event store.
contracts.rationale: All orchestration paths must make the same claim about when a terminal label is true.

contracts.shape: Durable command with operation_id, idempotency_key, actor, release/track/slice target, command kind, expected_status_revision, requested_at, and authorization context. Duplicate idempotency keys return the original operation; stale revisions return a typed conflict without mutation.
contracts.rationale: Operator intent must be transport-independent and safe to retry across process and network boundaries.

contracts.shape: Append-only event with monotonically ordered repository-local sequence, stable event ID, operation ID when applicable, timestamp, actor, target identity, event kind, public-safe detail, and source status revision. Events never contain credentials, model payloads, request/response bodies, or local worktree paths.
contracts.rationale: Monitoring, recovery, notification, and audit require one durable lifecycle account rather than surface-specific logs.

contracts.shape: Validated Baton status record plus authoritative source_ref and lowercase SHA-256 revision of canonical record bytes. CompareAndWrite requires the expected revision, validates the next state, writes a secure temporary file, syncs, atomically renames, and returns the new revision; a stale revision returns ErrConflict.
contracts.rationale: Content revisions provide cross-process conflict detection without changing Baton slice-status-v1.

contracts.shape: Stable event-derived notification with notification_id, operation_event_id, destination kind/reference, attention class, safe title/body, optional canonical board URL, state pending|delivering|delivered|retry_wait|dead, attempt count, next_attempt_at, delivered_at, and bounded error classification. It contains no mutation instruction or secret value.
contracts.live_test: S12 withholds an HTTP response, observes a durable retry, restores the endpoint, and observes exactly one delivered notification for the event.
contracts.rationale: Delivery failure must be observable and replayable without repeating the underlying operation.

contracts.surface: operations API v1 under /api/v1 plus GET /events stream
contracts.shape: Read endpoints return release/track/slice snapshots with source_ref and revision, liveness, blockers, operations, events, and notification health. Command endpoints accept control-command-v1 and require authentication, allowed origin/CSRF token, idempotency key, and If-Match status revision. SSE resumes from a stable event sequence. Errors are typed JSON and never optimistic 2xx.
contracts.rationale: The web UI and future transports need one versioned safe boundary over the same control service.

contracts.shape: Durable standing delegation naming release scope, integration target, granting actor, granted_at, expires_at, and optional revoked_at. Automatic integration merge requires an unexpired unrevoked matching delegation and all normal ship gates; otherwise the terminal operation is ready_to_merge and exits non-zero for an attempted automatic merge.
contracts.rationale: Operational autonomy must not silently consume release authority.

contracts.surface: operations HTTP bind, authentication, origin, CSRF, and mutation-precondition policy
contracts.shape: Loopback bind is the default. Non-loopback bind requires valid authentication and an explicit allowed-origin set. State-changing requests require authenticated authorization, an allowed Origin, a CSRF token bound to the session, an Idempotency-Key, and If-Match with the current state revision before command enqueue. Safe response headers deny framing and content sniffing; credentials are never accepted in query strings.
contracts.rationale: The API shape is not safe unless the HTTP edge between browser and handler has one owned policy.

contracts.shape: application/json projection of notification-envelope-v1 containing notification_id, operation_event_id, attention class, safe title/body, event timestamp, and optional canonical board URL. X-Sworn-Event-ID is stable across retries. When a destination signing secret is configured, X-Sworn-Signature carries an HMAC-SHA256 over the exact body. No command, credential, payload body, response body, or local path is sent.
contracts.rationale: Generic webhook consumers need a stable retry/signature contract rather than provider-specific payloads.

contracts.surface: outbound notification URL, redirect, credential, timeout, and response policy
contracts.shape: Destination URLs are absolute HTTP(S), have no userinfo or fragment, and are validated before persistence. Redirects are rejected. Credentials/signing secrets are destination-scoped and never forwarded to another host. Each attempt and total delivery have explicit deadlines. Diagnostics record bounded classification/status only and never response bodies or secret values.
contracts.rationale: The payload contract is not safe if redirects, credentials, and deadlines are left to the process default client.

contracts.shape: ntfy-compatible notification carrying safe title/body/tags and at most one view action to the canonical authenticated board URL. The action has no mutation query or command payload. Provider authentication is destination-scoped under C-10; notification_id/operation_event_id remain stable across retries.
contracts.rationale: Mobile paging preserves the useful phone notification gesture without creating a second control protocol.

contracts.shape: Opaque bounded session reference with HttpOnly, SameSite=Strict, Path=/, explicit Max-Age, and Secure whenever TLS is used. It is never accepted from a URL or browser storage and never echoed in API/event/log payloads.
contracts.rationale: Browser authentication attributes are an edge contract shared by the board and command handlers.

contracts.shape: Session-bound unpredictable token required on every state-changing operation request in addition to an exact allowed Origin. It is issued only through an authenticated same-origin response and is never persisted in events, URLs, or browser storage.
contracts.rationale: Authentication alone does not protect browser mutation requests from cross-site submission.

contracts.shape: Required lowercase SHA-256 C-04 status revision for a targeted state-changing request. Missing or stale values return a typed non-2xx precondition conflict with the current safe revision and enqueue no command.
contracts.rationale: The browser representation must not overwrite a newer loop-owned state.

contracts.shape: Required clean client-generated key scoped to authenticated actor, command kind, and target. Reuse with the same command returns the original operation; reuse with different content is a typed conflict. The key contains no secret and maps to exactly one C-02 operation.
contracts.rationale: Network/client replay must not duplicate a durable control operation.
